generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance AutonomousVehicleChassisLower : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Lower-level chassis for steering and speed control via Vedder Electronic Speed Controller in the 1/10th-scale autonomous vehicle"
]

instance AutonomousVehicleChassisUpper : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Upper-level chassis hosting autonomy hardware including NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano for processing autonomy components"
]

instance MotorActuator : DTDFVocab:Actuator [
    base:desc "Motor actuator for speed control in the autonomous vehicle chassis"
    DTDFVocab:producedData SpeedControllerFeedbackData
]

instance SteeringActuator : DTDFVocab:Actuator [
    base:desc "Steering servo actuator for directional control in the autonomous vehicle chassis"
    DTDFVocab:producedData SteeringAngleData
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance NvidiaJetsonNXSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "NVIDIA Jetson NX embedded processor for real-time data processing in the autonomous vehicle"
    DTDFVocab:producedData JetsonNXProcessedData
]

instance NvidiaJetsonOrinNanoSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano processor for compute offloading in autonomy applications"
    DTDFVocab:producedData OrinNanoProcessedData
]

instance HokuyoUST10LXLiDAR : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Hokuyo UST-10LX LiDAR for 3D object detection in the autonomous vehicle"
    DTDFVocab:producedData LiDARPointCloudData
]

instance IntelDeepsenseD435iCamera : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Intel Deepsense D435i Depth Camera for environmental perception in the autonomous vehicle"
    DTDFVocab:producedData DepthCameraData
]

instance VedderSpeedController : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Vedder Electronic Speed Controller for speed regulation in the autonomous vehicle chassis"
    DTDFVocab:producedData SpeedControllerFeedbackData
]

instance SteeringServoSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Steering servo sensor providing real-time steering angle data"
    DTDFVocab:producedData SteeringAngleData
]

// C4: Data Transmission
instance JetsonNXProcessedData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom NvidiaJetsonNXSensor
]

instance OrinNanoProcessedData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom NvidiaJetsonOrinNanoSensor
]

instance LiDARPointCloudData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HokuyoUST10LXLiDAR
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom IntelDeepsenseD435iCamera
]

instance DepthCameraData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom IntelDeepsenseD435iCamera
]

instance SpeedControllerFeedbackData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VedderSpeedController
]

instance SteeringAngleData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SteeringServoSensor
]

// C5: Data Models
instance ObjectTrackData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Object tracks (position/velocity) generated via DBSCAN + Kalman filter in the Physical Twin Entity"
    DTDFVocab:fromData LiDARPointCloudData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DBSCANProcessingEnabler
    DTDFVocab:inputTo KalmanFilterProcessingEnabler
]

instance EgoVehicleStateData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Ego vehicle state data (speed, steering angle) for the autonomous vehicle"
    DTDFVocab:fromData SpeedControllerFeedbackData
    DTDFVocab:fromData SteeringAngleData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2DataBusEnabler
]

instance CADVehicleModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "3D CAD representation of the autonomous vehicle for visualization"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo Custom3DVisualizationFE
]

instance CADEnvironmentModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "3D CAD representation of the vehicle environment for visualization"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo Custom3DVisualizationFE
]

instance AccControlAlgorithm : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "ACC algorithms (ISO 15622) implemented in the Controlling Functional Entity"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MATLABSimulinkEnabler
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2ControlPackage
]

instance HistoricalDataStorage : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "SQL database storing object tracks and ego vehicle state for historical analysis"
    DTDFVocab:fromData ObjectTrackData
    DTDFVocab:fromData EgoVehicleStateData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SQLDatabaseEnabler
]

// C6: Enablers
instance DBSCANProcessingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "DBSCAN clustering for object detection from LiDAR point clouds"
    DTDFVocab:enables ObjectTrackData
]

instance KalmanFilterProcessingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Kalman filter tracking for object position/velocity estimation"
    DTDFVocab:enables ObjectTrackData
]

instance ROS2DataBusEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "ROS2 middleware for inter-component communication between physical and digital twin"
    DTDFVocab:enables ObjectTrackData
    DTDFVocab:enables EgoVehicleStateData
]

instance MATLABSimulinkEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MATLAB/Simulink for implementing ACC algorithms and digital twinning functionalities"
    DTDFVocab:enables AccControlAlgorithm
]

instance ROS2ControlPackage : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "ROS2 packages implementing ACC control logic for the autonomous vehicle"
    DTDFVocab:enables AccControlAlgorithm
]

instance SQLDatabaseEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SQL database for storing object tracks and ego vehicle state data"
    DTDFVocab:enables HistoricalDataStorage
]

instance Custom3DVisualizationFE : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Custom 3D Visualization Functional Entity for real-time rendering of vehicle and environment"
    DTDFVocab:enables CADVehicleModel
    DTDFVocab:enables CADEnvironmentModel
    DTDFVocab:enables ObjectTrackData
    DTDFVocab:enables EgoVehicleStateData
]

instance ISO23247DataTranslation : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "ISO 23247-compliant Data Translation Functional Entity for standardized message serialization"
    DTDFVocab:enables LiDARPointCloudData
    DTDFVocab:enables DepthCameraData
    DTDFVocab:enables SpeedControllerFeedbackData
    DTDFVocab:enables SteeringAngleData
]

instance NVIDIAJetpack50SDK : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "NVIDIA Jetpack 5.0 SDK for Jetson integration and hardware platform support"
    DTDFVocab:enables NvidiaJetsonNXSensor
    DTDFVocab:enables NvidiaJetsonOrinNanoSensor
]

// C7: Services
instance DataMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time visualization of ego vehicle state (speed, steering angle), object tracks, and projected driving path"
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeVisualizationInsight
]

instance ComputationOffloadingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Offloading ACC control logic from vehicle ECUs to cloud-based compute (NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano)"
    DTDFVocab:provides PerformanceOptimizationAction
]

instance RemoteManagementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Enabling operators to enable/disable ACC, set desired speeds, and trigger emergency braking"
    DTDFVocab:provides ControlPanelAction
    DTDFVocab:provides EmergencyBrakingAction
]

instance ReportingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Generating reports for post-operation analysis via Reporting Functional Entity"
    DTDFVocab:provides HistoricalAnalysisInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance RealTimeVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time visualization of ego vehicle state (speed, steering angle), object tracks, and projected driving path"
]

instance PerformanceOptimizationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Optimizing ACC control logic performance via cloud-based compute offloading"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ControlPanelAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Dynamic adjustment of adaptive cruise control settings via Control Panel Functional Entity"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance EmergencyBrakingAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Remote emergency braking commands for human-in-the-loop safety"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance HistoricalAnalysisInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Historical data analysis for operational optimization (e.g., fuel economy improvements)"
]

// C18: Environment
instance AutonomousVehicleEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains AutonomousVehicleChassisLower
    base:contains AutonomousVehicleChassisUpper
    base:contains MotorActuator
    base:contains SteeringActuator
    base:contains NvidiaJetsonNXSensor
    base:contains NvidiaJetsonOrinNanoSensor
    base:contains HokuyoUST10LXLiDAR
    base:contains IntelDeepsenseD435iCamera
    base:contains VedderSpeedController
    base:contains SteeringServoSensor
]

instance DigitalTwinEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains CADVehicleModel
    base:contains CADEnvironmentModel
    base:contains ObjectTrackData
    base:contains EgoVehicleStateData
    base:contains AccControlAlgorithm
    base:contains HistoricalDataStorage
    base:contains DBSCANProcessingEnabler
    base:contains KalmanFilterProcessingEnabler
    base:contains ROS2DataBusEnabler
    base:contains MATLABSimulinkEnabler
    base:contains ROS2ControlPackage
    base:contains SQLDatabaseEnabler
    base:contains ISO23247DataTranslation
    base:contains Custom3DVisualizationFE
]

instance UserInterfaceEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains DataMonitoringService
    base:contains ComputationOffloadingService
    base:contains RemoteManagementService
    base:contains ReportingService
    base:contains RealTimeVisualizationInsight
    base:contains PerformanceOptimizationAction
    base:contains ControlPanelAction
    base:contains EmergencyBrakingAction
    base:contains HistoricalAnalysisInsight
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The DT’s Digital Twin Entity’s ACC Controlling FE computes desired speeds based on lead vehicle distance (per ISO 15622) and sends actuation commands via the Physical Twin Entity’s Actuation FE to the motor and steering servos. These commands are transmitted over Wi-Fi to the vehicle’s onboard hardware, where they are converted into motor voltage commands via the Actuation FE. Emergency braking commands are also remotely triggered through the User Entity’s Control Panel FE."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The DT operates at a **real-time scale** with a synchronization frequency of **100ms end-to-end latency** (per 3GPP TS 22.185) between the vehicle and DT Entity. Clock synchronization is maintained within **±100ms accuracy** across distributed devices to ensure event ordering and data integrity, though hardware limitations restrict stricter synchronization (e.g., <1ms) required by some vehicular standards."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The DT constellation is orchestrated via a **three-entity architecture** (ISO 23247-based): **Physical Twin Entity** (hosts sensors/actuators), **Digital Twin Entity** (processes data/controls ACC), and **User Entity** (provides visualization/management interfaces). The **Data Translation FE** acts as a cross-system intermediary, ensuring inter-entity communication via Wi-Fi (UDP/ROS2 topics). The system is **monolithic** in structure, with no sub-twin hierarchies, though the User Interface FE is decomposed into visualization and control panels."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The DT spans **three lifecycle stages**: **1) Design Phase** – Used for cyber-physical experimentation (e.g., ACC simulation, 3D CAD integration); **2) Manufacturing Phase** – Enables predictive maintenance (e.g., brake system monitoring via CREO Simulate integration); **3) Operational Phase** – Supports real-time optimization (e.g., fuel economy improvements via DT data analytics). The DT covers the **operated** phase of the physical twin’s lifecycle, focusing on runtime performance validation and remote management."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "Fidelity is ensured via: **1) Real-time Data Validation** – Sensor data (LiDAR, cameras) is pre-processed (DBSCAN + Kalman filter) to eliminate noise before transmission; **2) Latency Constraints** – End-to-end latency <100ms guarantees timely control commands; **3) Verification/Validation** – ACC logic adheres to ISO 15622 standards; **4) Uncertainty Handling** – Object tracks are probabilistic (Kalman filter) to account for sensor noise. Validation occurs during development (e.g., ROS2 testing) and operational phases (e.g., emergency braking testing)."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The DT employs a **hybrid network architecture**: **1) Onboard Communication** – ROS2 topics over ROS2 middleware for intra-entity (Physical Twin/Digital Twin) communication; **2) Inter-Entity Communication** – Wi-Fi (UDP) for cross-entity (Digital Twin/User) data exchange; **3) Protocols** – ISO 23247-compliant Data Translation FE for standardized message serialization (e.g., OPC UA for data translation); **4) Synchronization** – NTP-based clock synchronization (±100ms accuracy) for distributed devices. Security is implicit via ROS2’s built-in trust models."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The DT is deployed on **three hardware layers**: **1) Onboard Hardware** – NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano (Digital Twin Entity) and Jetson NX (Physical Twin Entity) for real-time processing; **2) Cloud Hosting** – External SQL databases for data storage; **3) User Interface** – Hosted on a remote server for visualization/management. Deployment follows ISO 23247’s entity separation principles, with each FE deployed as a ROS2 package."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The DT integrates horizontally with **external systems** via: **1) Standardized Protocols** – OPC UA (for data translation) and ROS2 (for inter-DT communication); **2) Data Exchange** – Object tracks and ego vehicle state are shared with other DTs or IoT systems via Wi-Fi; **3) Lifecycle Integration** – Enables digital threads for product lifecycle management (e.g., linking design, manufacturing, and operational data). Horizontal integration is not explicitly detailed but implied via ISO 23247’s cross-system entity design."
]

instance data_ownership_and_privacy : DTDFVocab:DataOwnershipPrivacy [
    base:desc "Data ownership is **shared**: **1) Physical Twin Owner** – Controls sensor data collection (e.g., LiDAR inputs); **2) DT Service Provider** – Manages data storage, visualization, and remote management. Privacy is addressed via: **1) Encrypted Wi-Fi Communication** (implicit in ROS2/Wi-Fi); **2) Access Controls** – User Entity restricts commands to authorized operators; **3) Anonymization** – Object tracks are processed to remove PII before storage. No explicit policies are outlined, but ISO 23247’s modular design supports privacy-by-design principles."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The DT adheres to the following standards: **1) ISO 23247** – Reference architecture (entity-based design, Data Translation FE); **2) ISO 15622** – ACC control logic (performance class I); **3) ROS2** – Middleware for inter-component communication; **4) 3GPP TS 22.185** – Latency requirements (V2V communication); **5) NVIDIA Jetpack 5.0 SDK** – Hardware abstraction for Jetson devices. The system is not fully compliant with ISO 23247’s Data Storage FE or stricter clock synchronization (<100ms) due to hardware constraints."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "Security and safety are addressed via: **1) Safety-Critical Design** – ACC logic adheres to ISO 15622, with emergency braking commands prioritized; **2) Latency Constraints** – <100ms end-to-end latency ensures timely control responses; **3) Remote Management** – Human-in-the-loop (Fully Autonomous Digital Twin with HITL) mitigates autonomous risks; **4) Data Integrity** – Clock synchronization (±100ms) and Data Translation FE prevent message corruption; **5) Access Controls** – User Entity restricts unauthorized commands. The system is **not explicitly certified** for autonomous driving (e.g., Level 4+), but safety is ensured via real-time validation and operator oversight."
]
